business.title: Shriram Properties expects Rs 600 cr revenue from upcoming JV project in Bengaluru
business.summary: Shriram Properties Ltd is developing a new housing project in Bengaluru. The company has signed an agreement for a 9.1-acre land parcel. This project is expected to generate over Rs 600 crore in revenue. The development will span 6.7 lakh sq ft. Shriram Properties plans to launch the project this financial year.

business.title: TDI Infratech to invest Rs 75 cr in upcoming Ramada hotel in Haryana
business.summary: TDI Infratech is pouring Rs 75 crore into an opulent hotel project in Panipat, Haryana, set to bolster the region's hospitality scene. The distinguished Wyndham Hotels & Resorts will oversee the management of this luxurious 53-room establishment, nestled within the TDI Connaught Estate.

The milestone highlights strong investor confidence, robust momentum and growing optimism around select commodity-linked stocks amid a broader market rally.

business.title: Infrastructure Investment Trusts distribute Rs 22,800cr in FY26 to unitholders
business.summary: Infrastructure Investment Trusts distributed nearly Rs 22,800 crore to unitholders last fiscal. Assets Under Management grew to Rs 7.1 lakh crore. The unitholder base saw a 64 percent increase. This indicates growing investor confidence in InvITs as a stable income source. InvITs also raised significant funds through equity, further strengthening the sector.

business.title: Rupee closes at 94.56 vs USD; US-Iran peace agreement details, Fed guidance awaited
business.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened for the third day in a row. This rise was supported by positive sentiment and anticipation of a U.S.-Iran peace agreement. Lower oil prices also boosted the rupee. Traders are now awaiting details of the peace deal and guidance from the U.S. Federal Reserve. The rupee is expected to continue its appreciation in the near term.

Assets under management rose to Rs 7.1 lakh crore, while market cap and investor participation expanded, reflecting rising confidence in infrastructure-linked investment vehicles.

business.title: Liotech Industries Rs 36-cr IPO to open on Wednesday
business.summary: Liotech Industries is launching its Rs 36 crore initial public offering on June 17. The bidding will be open until June 19. Shares are expected to list on the BSE SME on June 24. The company plans to use the funds for machinery, debt repayment, and working capital. This will help Liotech Industries expand its manufacturing and geographical reach.

business.title: IPO: 23 firms launch public issues worth Rs 27,000 cr in 2026; 236 proposals remain in pipeline
business.summary: India's IPO market saw a slowdown in 2026 following a robust 2025. However, activity is picking up with several companies set to launch public issues this month. The pipeline for mainboard IPOs remains strong. Domestic investor participation continues to be resilient, offering a counterbalance to volatile foreign investor sentiment. This indicates a positive outlook for the primary market.

business.title: Higher prices for gas, groceries and flights will outlast Iran war, experts say

business.title: World’s 500 richest add record $336 billion to wealth in one day
business.summary: The world's richest saw a massive gain of $336 billion on June 15. Elon Musk's wealth surged significantly, boosted by SpaceX's strong market debut. This single day marked the largest ever increase for the top 500 individuals. Their collective net worth reached a record $13.3 trillion.

business.title: Indian pharma leaving generics era behind, but the Street hasn't caught up yet: Nandan Kulkarni
business.summary: Indian pharma is shifting from generics to innovation. This transformation, extending to 2035, is creating significant new opportunities. Companies are investing in advanced research and development. Global regulatory changes favor Indian players. The market is not yet fully recognizing this innovation pivot and the quality of talent driving it.  GLP-1 drugs and supply chain diversification also present substantial growth potential.

business.title: Explained: Why Japan's Nikkei soared to lifetime high even after BOJ hiked interest rates to 31-year high
business.summary: Japan's Nikkei index surged past 70,000 for the first time as the Bank of Japan raised its short-term policy rate to 1%, the highest in 31 years. The central bank's gradual tightening, coupled with diminished Middle East conflict risks, supported equities. Simultaneously, an Iran-US peace deal boosted sentiment, leading to gains in India's Sensex and Nifty.

business.title: SpaceX set to surpass Amazon's market cap as post-IPO rally continues
business.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is experiencing a significant surge in its stock value. Shares rose over 10% in premarket trading on Tuesday. This rally puts SpaceX on track to surpass Amazon's market capitalization. It is poised to become the world's fifth-largest company. Trading volumes for SpaceX shares were exceptionally high.

Vedanta to be removed from MSCI Global Standard Indexes from June 22
business.summary: Vedanta shares will be removed from MSCI Global Standard Indexes from June 22. This follows the company's mega demerger into five separate listed entities. The residual Vedanta now has a smaller market capitalization. The demerger was completed on Monday with four new businesses debuting on the stock market. This index rejig may cause some share price volatility.
